title: How old were you when the first rebuild came out in 2007 and how old are you now
display_title: How old were you when the first rebuild came out in 2007 and how old are you now
wikitext:
[]: How old were you when the first rebuild came out in 2007 and how old are you now?             Quote Post by Thesufferingpumpkin » Tue Jan 19, 2021 8:39 pm 18 when 1.0 came out 31 and still waiting for 3.0+1.0, most likely will be 32 by the time it releases Lol

[]: Re: How old were you when the first rebuild came out in 2007 and how old are you now?             Quote Post by Akko » Tue Jan 19, 2021 10:16 pm Wasn't exactly into anime back then but well, 5 years old in 2007, turning 19 in 2021

[]: Re: How old were you when the first rebuild came out in 2007 and how old are you now?             Quote Post by Gus Hanson » Tue Jan 19, 2021 10:35 pm I was 19 when the first film came out. I will be 33 on February 18th and still waiting for Shin Eva if it hasn't come out by then.

[]: Re: How old were you when the first rebuild came out in 2007 and how old are you now?             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed Jan 20, 2021 6:04 am ugh please dont remind me I remember singing "Beautiful world" to my newborn baby sister. She's 12 now. She's taller than me. Just a few more years and she could go & watch EVA herself lol

[]: Re: How old were you when the first rebuild came out in 2007 and how old are you now?             Quote Post by JoelcrNeto » Wed Jan 20, 2021 6:10 am I was 11 when 1.0 came out, but I still didn't know EVANGELION. I'm now 25 years old.

[]: Re: How old were you when the first rebuild came out in 2007 and how old are you now?             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed Jan 20, 2021 6:21 am I was kinda lucky in that 1.0 came out soon after i discovered EVA in the first place like "woo more content yay~" I'm 27 now and i was 13 then. I'm not sure if I was still the tallest sibling then, my bro was taller at 11 but then i had a growth spurt at 12, but then he got his own and I've more or less stayed the same size since I was 14 or so. Between 11 and 15 I decided to wear all black & first the oldest of my sister freaked out a bit cause she thought she'd get all the black clothes as hand me downs, but I stayed short while she got super tall & we ended up being very different shapes. At least the teachers stopped mixing us up then. I kinda hoped to have more money or professional success by now...

[]: Re: How old were you when the first rebuild came out in 2007 and how old are you now?             Quote Post by JoelcrNeto » Wed Jan 20, 2021 6:55 am Kendrix wrote: I kinda hoped to have more money or professional success by now...

[]: Re: How old were you when the first rebuild came out in 2007 and how old are you now?             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Jan 20, 2021 8:36 am 1.0 was released a few days before my 50th birthday. I didn't expect to have already retired before the last one came out.

[]: Re: How old were you when the first rebuild came out in 2007 and how old are you now?             Quote Post by pwhodges » Wed Jan 20, 2021 9:00 am 61/74 I guess I'm another outlier, along with Mr. Tines. But it was more or less at the time I first got interested in anime at all (starting with NGE, in fact).

[]: Re: How old were you when the first rebuild came out in 2007 and how old are you now?             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed Jan 20, 2021 9:42 am I was 20 years old and a snob in film school when Eva Jo hit theaters in February, but I hadn't been convinced into liking anime yet until the summer of that year, so maybe that doesn't count? But that friend who drew me into the Dark Side knew exactly what would pull of film snob in this 20's into anime, and I was immediately given Akira to watch. Later on he showed me Nadia . I was hooked on whatever those were, so the jump from there to Evangelion wasn't quite that big. I think I started watching the TV series in 2008 with those bootleg 10-minute uploads on YouTube. It was pretty wild, and I ended up buying the boxset in late 2008/early 2009. I bought the D&R and EoE DVDs in 2010. I distinctly remember going on IMDb to see what the whole of the Eva series was like, and saw the first two " Rebuild of Evangelion " films listed as already produced, with the last two listed as still being in production. ( remember thinking, "Man, it's just like whatever anime is to have something really good like Eva be milked by other studios. Who is this "Studio Khara," and what are they doing with Gainax's property?" But sometime after lurking on the forums and eventually joining in 2009, I slowly learned that "Oh, it's the same creative team. These new films could still be good!" Evangelion 1.01: You Are (Not) Alone wasn't released in the United States until 2009, and I remember picking that up along with Utada's new English album at a local Walmart in early 2010, I think. So I really can't say I've been waiting since 2007 for Shin Eva, since I wasn't even interested in anime at the time, and I've only seen Eva Jo in 2010. It's only been a 10-year journey for me, but I guess that's still a good chunk of time. Anywho, I'm 33 now, staring down 34 come September. Life's had its ups and downs, and I'm still trying to figure out how I want to deal with the creative/technical industry. The year 2020 has really made me realize some things about myself that need work if I'm gonna actually dedicate my career to filmmaking. Can't wait for Shin Eva, though!

[]: Re: How old were you when the first rebuild came out in 2007 and how old are you now?             Quote Post by Grahsrihajul » Wed Jan 20, 2021 9:54 am 21 then, 34 now. We've been waiting quite a while for this last movie, haven't we? I can barely imagine what life without waiting for the movie will be like.

[]: Re: How old were you when the first rebuild came out in 2007 and how old are you now?             Quote Post by Javi2541997 » Wed Jan 20, 2021 10:09 am I was 10 years when 1.11 came out. Now, I am 23 years and will be 24 in April

[]: Re: How old were you when the first rebuild came out in 2007 and how old are you now?             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Wed Jan 20, 2021 1:46 pm I was 11.

[]: Re: How old were you when the first rebuild came out in 2007 and how old are you now?             Quote Post by BernardoCairo » Wed Jan 20, 2021 4:28 pm I was 5 years old when 1.0 came out. But, the first time I watched it, I was nine years old or something. I couldn't understand anything. As I said in another post, after some time, the only things I could remember from it were the destruction Antarctica by angels, the "global warming" elements and that there was a penguin in the film. In retrospect, the movie was not complicated to understand, but I had a very closed mind for anime at that time. In fact, for anything that came from Japan and wasn't video games. I really can't say why... Therefore, I didn't even try to pay attention to what was happening on screen. Years later I became interested in anime (through Dragon Ball and Akira), watched Neon Genesis Evangelion, liked it a lot and revisited this movie. I really liked it (I'm 18 now).

[]: Re: How old were you when the first rebuild came out in 2007 and how old are you now?             Quote Post by RCM88 » Fri Jan 22, 2021 3:32 am 19 when 1.0 came out. 32/33 depending when 3.0 + 1.0 releases this year.

[]: Re: How old were you when the first rebuild came out in 2007 and how old are you now?             Quote Post by Line » Fri Jan 22, 2021 8:44 am 9 years old. Those time feel like yesterday. It passes so quickly. I feel so old

[]: Re: How old were you when the first rebuild came out in 2007 and how old are you now?             Quote Post by GunmetalSnail429 » Fri Jan 22, 2021 6:50 pm 17 in 2007 30 now. Anno, it has been almost HALF my lifetime.

[]: Re: How old were you when the first rebuild came out in 2007 and how old are you now?             Quote Post by Blockio » Sat Jan 23, 2021 11:33 am I was 8 when Jo first came out, but I didn't get into Eva until I was 16 (I think? Might be off by a year), now I'm checks calendar 21

[]: Re: How old were you when the first rebuild came out in 2007 and how old are you now?             Quote Post by Zusuchan » Sat Jan 23, 2021 1:16 pm I was 3 years old when Jo came out and got into Eva just some time after turning 16. Which also means that unlike some other people here, I won't have to wait that long for Shin, hehe...

[]: Re: How old were you when the first rebuild came out in 2007 and how old are you now?             Quote Post by BusterMachine4 » Sat Jan 23, 2021 1:50 pm Wait, you're 16 too? Guess it's time to reveal my true age, then. I was worried people would look down on me because I'm not an adult yet. But yeah, I was just 2 when Rebuild 1.0 was released. I didn't even know how to read yet! This series really has been going on for a long time. Thankfully though, I just got into the franchise last year, so I haven't had to wait too long.
